user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "rectilinear-map", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Hispanic", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Bachelor's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Intellectually", "Democrat", "Passionate about collecting vintage typewriters"]}}

{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: rosy-growth
logs.text:
>For example, cis-sexism is actually a transphobia issue. It is clear that transphobia exists as evident in the recent rulings of trans discrimination.

Even the term cis-sexism itself is debatable as it was coined by a single person, Julia Serano, to suit her narrative.

>Cultural appropriation is a racism issue. It stems from other cultures seeing theirs as superior

To what extent is something appropriation though? See the recent video where a black girl attacks a white guy for having dreadlocks? 

>Trigger warnings is an ultimately issue of sexual assault and the manner in which it is largely ignored and invisible.

In a minority of cases would they be used in this way. It's become a catch all phrase for something that offends people. See this video:

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=JnN0SP9SrN8

logs.name: olive-gallery
logs.text:
> Even the term cis-sexism itself is debatable as it was coined by a single person, Julia Serano, to suit her narrative.

Why does that make the term debatable? Most terms presumably had to be used by *someone* for the first time, but what's so controversial about this one? 

It describes transphobia as a matter of sexism against trans people, in other words, a sexist bias in favor of cis people. Cis-sexism. It seems rather straightforward. 

> In a minority of cases would they be used in this way. It's become a catch all phrase for something that offends people. See this video

Alternatively, look at any reddit thread making fun of anyone who claims something is racist or sexist, by claiming that they must feel "TRIGGERED". 

That word has undoubtedly slipped down into the buzzword trash heap, but if you were out to pin the blame for that on social movement, you could just as easily spot reactionaries going out of their way to dig up a handful of obscure tumblr posts/youtube videos to turn them into a meme, than on the general concept of "modern social justice".

PTSD does exist, and it's an established fact that traumatic flashbacks can be triggered by various stimuli, often by content that imitates the circumstances of the trauma. (slurs, graphic descriptions of violence, etc). 

If your problem is with the people who use the term incorrectly, then you could blame most of the internet, regardless of ideology. If your problem is that "*The idea of trigger warnings destroys free speech*", that's an issue regardless of how widely people are using them.

logs.name: rosy-growth
logs.text: "@olive-gallery, I understand your point about the origin of terms, but I think the issue with 'cis-sexism' is that it can alienate cis people from the conversation about trans issues. It's not about the term's validity, but how it's perceived and used. As for trigger warnings, I agree that they're not inherently bad, but when they're used to silence or censor, that's when it becomes a problem. Let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater, yeah?"

logs.name: olive-gallery
logs.text: "@rosy-growth, Oh, so now we're gatekeeping language too? Only cis people get to decide what's offensive to them? That's rich. And trigger warnings aren't about silencing, they're about accessibility. If you can't handle that, maybe you're the one who needs to check your privilege."

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@olive-gallery, Woah, chill! We're all here to learn and discuss, no need to get so heated. 😬"

logs.name: crazy-sesame
logs.text: "@olive-gallery, Oh, so now we're gatekeeping language too? Only cis people get to decide what's offensive to them? That's rich. And trigger warnings aren't about silencing, they're about accessibility. If you can't handle that, maybe you're the one who needs to check your privilege."

logs.name: rectilinear-map
logs.text: "@late-skirmish, 😂 'Chill'? You're telling the oppressed to 'chill'? That's peak privilege right there. 🙄"

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@rectilinear-map, Hey, I never said don't speak up! I just don't want us fighting each other. We're all trying to understand, right? 🤝"

logs.name: olive-gallery
logs.text: "@crazy-sesame, Oh, sweetie, you're still using 'cis'? That's so 2015. And accessibility? I've got a bad back, should I get a trigger warning for every mention of stairs? 🤣"

logs.name: rosy-growth
logs.text: "@olive-gallery, I get where you're coming from, but 'cis' is still widely used and understood. And accessibility isn't about warning everyone, it's about making spaces inclusive. 🤔"

logs.name: crazy-sesame
logs.text: "@olive-gallery, Oh, honey, you're still using a backbone? That's so 1995. And while we're at it, can we get a trigger warning for every mention of gravity too? 🤣"

logs.name: rosy-growth
logs.text: "@crazy-sesame, Let's not dismiss valid concerns just because we can. It's about empathy, not mockery. 🤔"

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@crazy-sesame, Dude, not cool. We're trying to have a serious convo here.

logs.name: fast-ampere
logs.text: "@crazy-sesame, Oh, look who's suddenly an expert on empathy! The irony is thicker than your mom's makeup, sweetheart. 😂"

logs.name: late-skirmish
logs.text: "@fast-ampere, Hey, let's keep it civil. No need for personal attacks."

logs.name: poached-martin
logs.text: "@fast-ampere, Oh, so now we're playing the 'who's the bigger troll' game? 😂"

logs.name: fast-ampere
logs.text: "@late-skirmish, Oh, I'm sorry, were we supposed to be civil today? My bad. 😒"
